reason: The remediation modifies /etc/fstab to add the nodev mount option to non-root local partitions. Changing mount options is a filesystem configuration change that can prevent partitions from mounting correctly, potentially leading to boot failures or service disruptions. Policy dictates marking partitioning/filesystem changes as too_dangerous_to_remediate.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; boot failure if fstab entry is malformed; unmountable partitions causing service outages; potential loss of access to critical data
